input.prompt:
Migrate this EF Core Cosmos DB app to .NET 9.
Our existing Cosmos DB container has 500K documents. They were written by EF Core 8 and have
a "Discriminator" property and id values in format "Product|123".
We run direct SQL queries against the container that reference c.Discriminator.
Identify all breaking changes — especially around data compatibility with existing documents.

data.reasoningText: The user wants to migrate an EF Core Cosmos DB app from .NET 8 to .NET 9 and wants me to identify all breaking changes, especially around data compatibility with existing documents. Let me first look at the existing code files to understand the current setup.

data.content: Now I need to update the `DbContext` to handle the breaking changes. EF Core 9 for Cosmos introduces three critical breaking changes that affect your 500K documents:

toolCalls.name: edit
edit

data.content: Now let me add the value generator class to preserve the legacy `Product|123` ID format:

toolCalls.name: create

data.content:
Here's a summary of the **3 breaking changes** identified and mitigated:

| # | Breaking Change (EF Core 9) | Impact on Your 500K Docs | Fix Applied |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Discriminator renamed to `$type`** | Your raw SQL queries (`c.Discriminator`) would return nothing; new writes would use `$type`, creating a split dataset | Explicitly configured `.HasDiscriminator<string>("Discriminator")` to retain the old property name |
| 2 | **ID format simplified** — no longer `EntityType|Key` | EF Core 9 would generate `"123"` but existing docs have `"Product|123"`, making them unfindable | Added `LegacyCosmosIdGenerator` to preserve the `Product|123` format |
| 3 | **Synchronous I/O removed** | `ToList()`, `First()`, etc. now throw `InvalidOperationException` | Converted to `async`/`ToListAsync()` |

All changes preserve **full backward compatibility** with existing documents — no data migration needed.
